title: Thermodynamics Laws
definition: A set of four fundamental laws that govern energy, heat, and work in a system, formulated by Carnot, Clausius, Kelvin, and others.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The laws of thermodynamics explain why your coffee gets cold and your ice cream melts, and why we can't build a perpetual motion machine.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The laws of thermodynamics describe the principles that govern energy transformations, providing insights into engineering, chemistry, biology, and cosmology.
difficulty_levels.expert: Thermodynamics laws unite physics, chemistry, and biology by connecting energy transformations with temperature, entropy, and time.

extra_fields.insight: Thermodynamics provides the foundational principles that link energy, heat, and work across all scales and disciplines.
